# Transcrição: 04 Offline Event Tracking

- **Fonte:** 04 Offline Event Tracking
- **Duração:** 05:22
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Alright guys, so there's another really cool way to track purchases that Facebook ads have generated when and especially if your client doesn't sell online. So for example, maybe the sell takes place over the phone, maybe the sale takes place face-to-face, think car dealerships, think doctors, think chiropractors. There's a lot of businesses out there where the sale actually doesn't happen online. It's not e-commerce in that sense, right? So how do you track purchases? Especially if this is something that you're trying to optimize for, right? So you may be able to generate great leads, but how do you know which of those campaigns actually generated the clients so that you can shift budgets into that and also optimize for that activity? Well, it's called offline tracking conversions. Now you need to be using Business Manager to set this up guys. So when you're in your Business Manager, what you're going to do is click on this little hamburger tool and we're going to go all the way to offline events over here. If you haven't set this up yet, you'll see this little prompt. So we're going to click Get Started here. And what Facebook is going to ask you to do is to upload offline events. And then obviously it takes a moment to read through the terms, agree, and continue. And this is essentially a CSV file that we're going to upload and things that you want to make sure that you have in this file are things like the customer name, first name, email address, the value, what they paid for, as much information as you can really. So there's 17 identifiers that you can identify customers here with. So for example, these are email, phone number, first name, last name, zip code, city, date of birth. This is almost exactly what you would do to upload a custom event. Now the one difference here that we need to be aware of is that we need to have an event time. So Facebook needs to know when that purchase took place. So you need to have this row in your CSV file or the row that you're copying and pasting in here. So at the very least, you need this as well. So let's go copy and paste. And we're going to, ah, ah, ah, come on.

## [03:01 - 05:22]

And great. So you'll see here we've got event time. And we're going to click Upload. And then you're going to select which of the standard events that you want to associate with this. Now notice here how purchase is grayed out. That's because we didn't add the value amount. So if you want to use purchase, you need to have a value to the event. So let's just say, for example, lead in the situation and go continue. You'll see now we have an event that we've created. It's called Sales for TP. I don't even know if that's how you spell TP, but Sales for TP's. And when we go back into our ads manager, if we create a dummy campaign, what you will then see is this new little option here. So offline tracking. So this now tracks, allows us to track conversions that are being uploaded through our offline event set. So as you go through, just upload in your offline events, the customer lists that you have and add these to your offline event set. And Facebook will match them to the ads and the campaigns and the ad sets that generated those leads or purchases or customers. So this is a really cool tool. It obviously involves a little bit more manual work. There are advanced ways to automate this process. It's a little bit more intense to do that. If you're just uploading files at the end of the month, that's one way to do it or every week or every day, depending on how intense your customer is and how much ad spend is being spent. But this essentially allows you to track conversions that are perhaps taking place in a situation where you cannot track them online. So they're not taking place on a website or on Facebook. They're happening on a phone call or face to face.
